seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Smolagents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Smolagents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building a RAG Pipeline with Smolagents enables lightweight, code-driven document retrieval and synthesis using Hugging Face's simple agent framework. However, agent-generated code for vector search, context assembly, and LLM calls introduces risks like hallucinations, unsafe executions, and citation errors. Vex provides essential verification to ensure source faithfulness and context relevance in high-risk production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Smolagents
architectureOverview.description:
The Smolagents RAG pipeline starts with a retriever agent using vector search (e.g., FAISS or Pinecone) to fetch relevant document chunks from a knowledge base based on user queries. A context assembler agent then ranks and concatenates retrieved chunks, applying relevance filters before passing to a synthesizer agent. The synthesizer uses an LLM to generate responses with source attribution, often via code agents that execute embedding lookups and formatting logic.

Smolagents' code-based reasoning shines in dynamic retrieval but amplifies risks when agents generate Python for database queries or LLM prompts without safeguards. Multi-step flows compound errors across retrieval, assembly, and synthesis phases. Vex wraps these agent runs to verify outputs against source faithfulness and hallucination criteria.

Key components include embedding models (e.g., sentence-transformers), vector stores, and lightweight LLMs, all orchestrated via smolagents' simple agent.run() interface.

risks.name: Code agent generating unsafe Python execution
risks.description: Smolagents code agents may generate exec() calls or import dangerous modules during dynamic retrieval logic
risks.mitigation: Wrap agent.run() with vex.verify() using code_safety checks

risks.name: Tool agent misinterpreting tool descriptions
risks.description: Retriever agents misparse vector store tool schemas, returning irrelevant chunks
risks.mitigation: Vex context_relevance verification on retrieved documents

risks.name: Multi-step reasoning errors compounding
risks.description: Errors in retrieval propagate through assembly and synthesis, creating hallucinated citations
risks.mitigation: End-to-end source_faithfulness checks across pipeline stages

risks.name: Hallucination in synthesized responses
risks.description: LLM synthesis ignores provided context, fabricating facts despite retrieved sources

implementation.title: Setup Smolagents RAG Retriever
implementation.description: Initialize FAISS vector store and retriever agent with embedding model
implementation.code:
from smolagents import CodeAgent, tool
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
import faiss
import numpy as np

embedder = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')

documents = ["Doc1: AI safety...", "Doc2: RAG patterns..."]
embeddings = embedder.encode(documents)
dimension = embeddings.shape[1]
index = faiss.IndexFlatL2(dimension)
index.add(np.array(embeddings))

@tool
def retrieve_docs(query: str, top_k: int = 3) -> list:
    query_emb = embedder.encode([query])
    distances, indices = index.search(query_emb, top_k)
    return [documents[i] for i in indices[0]]

retriever = CodeAgent(tools=[retrieve_docs])

implementation.title: Context Assembly Agent
implementation.description: Rank and format retrieved chunks for LLM context
implementation.code:
from smolagents import Agent

def assemble_context(query: str, docs: list) -> str:
    ranked_docs = sorted(docs, key=lambda d: len(d.split()))  # Simple relevance proxy
    context = "\n".join([f"[DOC{i+1}] {doc}" for i, doc in enumerate(ranked_docs[:4])])
    return f"Query: {query}\nContext:\n{context}"

assembler = Agent(system_prompt="Assemble relevant context from retrieved docs")

implementation.title: RAG Synthesizer with Citations
implementation.description: Generate answer with source attribution markers

implementation.title: Full RAG Pipeline Orchestration
implementation.code:
def rag_pipeline(query: str):
    # Step 1: Retrieve
    docs = retriever.run(f"retrieve relevant docs for: {query}")
    
    # Step 2: Assemble
    context = assembler.run(f"assemble context for query: {query} from docs: {docs}")
    
    # Step 3: Synthesize
    answer = synthesizer.run(f"synthesize answer for: {query} using context: {context}")
    return answer

result = rag_pipeline("What is RAG?")

cta.heading: Secure Your Smolagents RAG Pipeline
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your agent.run() calls today to eliminate hallucinations and ensure production-grade reliability.
